inputs.name: accessibleDescription
inputs.description: Defines the accessible description of the component.

inputs.name: accessibleDescriptionRef
inputs.description: Receives id(or many ids) of the elements that describe the input.

inputs.name: accessibleName
inputs.description: Defines the accessible ARIA name of the component.

inputs.name: accessibleNameRef
inputs.description: Receives id(or many ids) of the elements that label the input.

inputs.name: disabled
inputs.description:
Defines whether the component is in disabled state.

*Note:** A disabled component is completely noninteractive.

inputs.name: filter
inputs.description: Defines the filter type of the component.

inputs.name: maxlength
inputs.description:
Sets the maximum number of characters available in the input field.

*Note:** This property is not compatible with the ui5-input type InputType.Number. If the ui5-input type is set to Number, the maxlength value is ignored.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description:
Determines the name by which the component will be identified upon submission in an HTML form.

*Note:** This property is only applicable within the context of an HTML Form element.
*Note:** When the component is used inside a form element,
the value is sent as the first element in the form data, even if it's empty.

inputs.name: noTypeahead
inputs.description: Defines whether the value will be autcompleted to match an item

inputs.name: open
inputs.description:
Defines whether the suggestions picker is open.
The picker will not open if the `showSuggestions` property is set to `false`, the input is disabled or the input is readonly.
The picker will close automatically and `close` event will be fired if the input is not in the viewport.

inputs.name: placeholder
inputs.description:
Defines a short hint intended to aid the user with data entry when the
component has no value.

inputs.name: readonly
inputs.description:
Defines whether the component is read-only.

*Note:** A read-only component is not editable,
but still provides visual feedback upon user interaction.

inputs.name: required
inputs.description: Defines whether the component is required.

inputs.name: showClearIcon
inputs.description: Defines whether the clear icon of the input will be shown.

inputs.name: showSuggestions
inputs.description: Defines whether the component should show suggestions, if such are present.

inputs.name: showValueHelpIcon
inputs.description:
Determines whether a value help icon will be visualized in the end of the input.
Pressing the icon will fire `value-help-trigger` event.

inputs.name: type
inputs.description:
Defines the HTML type of the component.

*Notes:**

- The particular effect of this property differs depending on the browser
and the current language settings, especially for type `Number`.
- The property is mostly intended to be used with touch devices
that use different soft keyboard layouts depending on the given input type.
- Type `Number` does not support suggestions.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description:
Defines the value of the component.

*Note:** The property is updated upon typing.

inputs.name: valueState
inputs.description: Defines the value state of the component.

outputs.name: ui5Change
outputs.description: Fired when the input operation has finished by pressing Enter or on focusout.

outputs.name: ui5Close
outputs.description: Fired when the suggestions picker is closed.

outputs.name: ui5Input
outputs.description:
Fired when the value of the component changes at each keystroke,
and when a suggestion item has been selected.

outputs.name: ui5Open
outputs.description: Fired when the suggestions picker is open.

outputs.name: ui5Select
outputs.description: Fired when some text has been selected.

outputs.name: ui5SelectionChange
outputs.description:
Fired when the user navigates to a suggestion item via the ARROW keys,
as a preview, before the final selection.

outputs.name: ui5TokenDelete
outputs.description: Fired when tokens are being deleted.

outputs.name: ui5ValueHelpTrigger
outputs.description:
Fired when the value help icon is pressed
and F4 or ALT/OPTION + ARROW_UP/ARROW_DOWN keyboard keys are used.
